title: Add Search and Launch Button to Unassigned Reports List
story: As a data analyst, I want to search the list of unassigned reports and open them in Salesforce, so that I can identify the right report to assign when managing hundreds or thousands of reports.
description:
For Salesforce instances with hundreds or thousands of reports, finding the right one to assign was difficult. Two features were added to the unassigned reports list:

1. **Search box**: Filter the unassigned reports list by keyword
2. **Launch button**: Open the Salesforce report in a new tab to verify it before assignment

The original row-click-to-assign behavior was replaced with a dedicated 'Add' button since the row now has two actions (launch and assign). This provides clear visual separation between the two actions.
acceptance_criteria: Search box filters the unassigned reports list by keyword
Each row has a launch link to open the report in Salesforce in a new tab
Each row has a distinct 'Add' button for assigning the report to a metric
Launch and assign actions do not interfere with each other
Add button uses a green 'Add' button style for clear affordance
ux_changes: Added search box and launch link to unassigned reports list. Changed row-click-to-assign to a dedicated 'Add' button for clear action separation. Green 'Add' button provides distinct visual contrast from the launch link.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation used a checkbox icon for assignment but was changed to a green 'Add' button for better clarity when two actions are on the same row. The row-click event was replaced with an icon/button-based assignment to avoid conflict with the launch link.
comment_summary: Developer initially used a checkbox icon for assignment but the row click event conflicted with the launch link. Changed to an icon-based approach, then to a green 'Add' button per reviewer feedback for better UX clarity. Final implementation with 'Add' button was approved.
